title: A Shady Friend For Torrid Days
content:
A shady friend for torrid days
    Is easier to find
    Than one of higher temperature
    For frigid hour of mind.

    The vane a little to the east
    Scares muslin souls away;
    If broadcloth breasts are firmer
    Than those of organdy,

    Who is to blame? The weaver?
    Ah! the bewildering thread!
    The tapestries of paradise
    So notelessly are made!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "A Shady Friend For Torrid Days", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
